target-mod-mol-instance-ref

(/moleculizer-input/model/reaction-gens/gpa-exchange-gen/target-mod-mol-instance-ref)

Here, you say which protein in the "enabling complex" is actually the G-protein. You do this by giving the instance name of the protein, which must be a mod-mol, since nucleotide bindings are modeled as modifications. In the mod-site-ref element, you say which modification site on the mod-mol is involved. Note that the G-protein (Gpa1) must already be bound to GDP (i.e. have the GDP-bound modification at the correct modification site) to be involved in this simplified form of nucleotide exchange.

AttributesChildren
  • name
  • mod-site-ref
